column1: Distant Calls in Fading Light;Christine Ward;FALSE;"Distant Calls in Fading Light is Christine Ward's debut monograph. Weaving together years of her practice across Australia
column2: Norway and the world
column3: the book is structured like a fairy tale - the viewer leaves home
column4: falls into somewhat of a new world. Equally inspiring and intimidating
column5: this is a deliberately moody collection of photographs that aim to showcase the common threads across Christine's work: appreciation of nature
column6: a celebration of the more intense parts of the world
column7: and an asthetic that is as unique as it is compelling.

column1: To Be Developed
column2: To Be Continued;Odette England;FALSE;"To Be Developed
column3: To Be Continued is a book about changing visibility as a tween becomes a teenager
column4: and the ever-changing relationship between mother and daughter
column6: who is seen and who is not. Through photographing her daughter/muse
column7: Odette England’s images are both collaborative and playful - with the photographer and her daughter often obscuring what can be seen and how easily it can be seen.

column1: Accompanied by England’s adaptation of text by Season Butler
column2: the work is not trying to bottle the moment or hold onto a fleeting period
column3: but is celebrating the changes in a person and their relationships to others and the world. Change is temporal and inevitable
column4: and for that reason is savoured here. The design of the book mimics this
column5: made all the more precious because it of the softness of construction and paper.

We Were Just Little Boys;Tace Stevens;FALSE;"Building on Tace Stevens’ commissioned series
column2: We Were Just Little Boys tells the story of the Survivors of Kinchela Boys Home. This state-run institution was the site of unimaginable trauma and pain
column3: as little boys were ripped from families and communities
column4: imprisoned in a school and had their culture denied.

column1: With written contributions from surviving Uncles
column2: and writing from the CEO of the Kinchela Boys Home Aboriginal Corporation
column3: this book is collaborative
column4: grief-filled and advocates for the need for deep healing.

column1: Tace was the winner of the Tall Poppy Press Open Call in 2024.
This book was made possible with support from Kinchela Boys Home Aboriginal Corporation
column2: Magnum Foundation and the World Monuments Fund.

column1: 50% of sale price is paid to the Kinchela Aboriginal Boys Home Corporation.

column1: Big Bug;Jake Nemirovsky;FALSE;"Big Bug is a book all about the imprecision of strong memories
column2: the fuzziness of something clear
column3: remembering the taste without being able to taste the memory. This work plays with how visuals can be the starting point for a more expansive recollection: a connection to something the viewer has touched
column4: tasted

column1: In this book
column2: Jake Nemirovsky uses the surface of an image to provoke deeper and more hidden perspectives. Free of themes or a singular narrative
column3: this book seeks to trigger memories
column4: recall experiences and serve as photo album for any viewer - tantilisingly close to experiences we each might recall

column1: Site Specific;Matt Dunne;FALSE;"Site Specific is a book that explores the ways photography can be exhibited. Casting a wide net

column1: Co-authored by Matt Dunne and Callum Beaney
column2: the book highlights methods as diverse as using post-it-notes as a medium to collage to traditional framing.

column1: Photographers featured include Trent Parke
column2: Dayanita Singh
column3: Ying Ang
column4: Heikki Kaski

column1: This book is a reference book for artists looking to exhibit

column2: it is part public grief and part true crime
column3: an investigation into the killing of Wedge-Tailed Eagles in Victoria
column4: Australia.

column1: The backstory: In 2017
column2: a man called the Police and reported that he had murdered more than 400 eagles over the previous two years
column3: all at the instruction of his boss. Meticulously recorded
column4: the ongoing investigation uncovered tremendous destruction of life in a far-flung corner of Australia's South-East. Far from isolated
column5: this crime is one often repeated throughout the country where people trap
column8: The vast distances hiding their actions from prying eyes. Matt Dunne has been following these crimes
column9: visiting the sites where Eagles have been killed. Tracing the psychology
column11: history and animal victims in work that is part true crime and part public grieving. It shows the continuation of much of Australia's worst history: destruction of native species in service of agricultural interests. The book ultimately asks 'what have we traded?
